new_classes.definition_en: Spirit obtained by distilling grape wine or grape marc (grape pomace), including wine distillate (raw distillate of wine) obtained by primary distillation after alcoholic fermentation of grape wine; does not possess the properties of neutral ethyl alcohol; classified under CN subheading 2208 20 (Chapter 22).

Process of distilling grape wine or grape marc (grape pomace) to produce grape spirit or wine distillate; each individual represents the unique grape-distillation process type in the CN Chapter 22 classification.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms capture the main distinguishing criteria for CN 2208 20: (1) the product is a spirit (subclass of eucn:Spirit), (2) it is produced by distillation of grape wine or grape marc (eucn:GrapeDistillation process), and (3) it is assigned cnHeadingCode '220820'. The legal text also notes that 'wine distillate' (raw distillate) is included and that the product does NOT have the properties of neutral ethyl alcohol — the disjointness with eucn:EthylAlcohol is already inherited from eucn:Spirit (which is owl:disjointWith eucn:EthylAlcohol in the TBox), so that criterion is covered structurally. The note references HS Explanatory Note third paragraph (1), which may contain further discriminating sub-criteria (e.g. specific alcoholic strength thresholds for brandy vs. raw distillate) that are not fully represented due to the truncated legal text provided, resulting in a slight coverage gap.
